images.caption: Figure 1:AffordanceVLA Overview.(Bottom-left)AffordanceVLA employs three specialized experts (Understanding, Affordance Generation, and Action), leveraging structured affordance forecasting (Which2Act, Where2Act, and How2Act) as intermediate representations to bridge perception and action.(Top)Enabled by a three-stage training strategy with progressive data curriculum,(Bottom-right)AffordanceVLA achieves strong performance across both simulation and real-world evaluations.

Figure 2:Pipeline.The framework employs a MoT architecture comprising three specialized experts of Understanding (‚Ñ≥u‚Äãn‚Äãd\mathcal{M}_{und}), Affordance Generation (‚Ñ≥g‚Äãe‚Äãn\mathcal{M}_{gen}), and Action (‚Ñ≥a‚Äãc‚Äãt\mathcal{M}_{act}), which are coordinated via a unidirectional Understanding-Affordance-Action (UAA) progressive attention mechanism. Given an RGB observationOtO_{t}and instructionll,‚Ñ≥u‚Äãn‚Äãd\mathcal{M}_{und}extracts fused semanticshtu‚Äãn‚Äãdh_{t}^{und}.‚Ñ≥g‚Äãe‚Äãn\mathcal{M}_{gen}then decodeshtu‚Äãn‚Äãdh_{t}^{und}into structured affordance tokensA^t\hat{A}_{t}(Which2Act,Where2Act,How2Act) as intermediate priors. Figure 6:Backbone Representation Evaluation.Performance of a fully trained VLA backbone (100k steps) when evaluated with decoders trained for fewer steps (5k to 100k). The backbone maintains robust feature extraction even when paired with a weakly trained decoder.

Figure 7:Qualitative Visualization of Where2Act Affordance Maps.The model demonstrates robust Vision-Language alignment, dynamically adjusting its affordance predictions based on varying language instructions given the identical visual observation.

Figure 8:Sequential execution of the ‚ÄúClean all the rubbish‚Äù task, alongside a demonstration of human intervention. The policy‚Äôs ability to recover from external disturbances highlights its strong generalizability and robustness.
